levels.description: Covers the CP-105-2024 VT Level I topical outline: introduction and history of visual testing, standard terminology, fundamentals of vision and lighting, visual testing equipment, employer-defined applications including inherent, processing, and service-induced discontinuities, and performing VT to specific procedures.

modules.title: Introduction, Definitions, and Fundamentals of Visual Testing

lessons.title: What Is Visual Testing? History, Applications, and Terminology
objectives: Define visual testing (VT) and summarize its history as an NDT method
Identify common industrial applications of VT
Use standard VT terms and their meanings correctly
topics: 2.0 Definitions: standard terms and their meanings in the employer's industry

media.title: Introduction to Visual Testing: The First NDT Method
VT Terminology Walkthrough
Glossary of Standard VT Terms

lessons.title: Fundamentals of Vision and Lighting
objectives: Describe how the human eye forms images and the basics of visual perception
Explain why adequate lighting is essential to a valid visual test
Recognize how vision limitations affect inspection reliability

media.title: How the Eye Sees: Vision Basics for Inspectors
Anatomy of the Eye and Image Formation
Light Level vs. Detection: Adjust the Lux

lessons.title: Material Attributes, Environmental Factors, and Direct vs. Indirect Methods
objectives: Explain how material attributes such as surface condition affect what an inspector can see
Identify environmental factors that influence visual test results
Distinguish direct from indirect (remote) visual testing methods

media.title: Direct vs. Indirect Visual Testing
Environmental and Material Variables That Affect VT
Surface Condition Comparison: Clean vs. Contaminated

lessons.title: Basic Optical Aids: Mirrors, Magnifiers, and Light Sources
objectives: Select appropriate mirrors and magnifiers for common inspection tasks
Describe light sources and special lighting used in visual testing
Demonstrate proper care and verification of basic optical aids

media.title: Using Mirrors and Magnifiers in the Field
Magnifier Types and Working Distances
Light Source Selection Guide

lessons.title: Borescopes, Fiberscopes, and Remote Visual Inspection Systems
objectives: Differentiate rigid borescopes, fiberscopes, and video borescopes
Describe when remote visual inspection (RVI) systems are required
Identify basic handling precautions for scope equipment

media.title: Borescope Family Tour: Rigid, Fiber, and Video
Guide a Video Borescope Through a Tube Bundle
Cutaway of a Fiberscope Optical Train

lessons.title: Gauges, Measuring Tools, and Automated/Computer-Enhanced Systems
objectives: Identify common gauges, templates, scales, micrometers, and calipers used in VT
Explain the role of automated and computer-enhanced visual inspection systems
Select the correct measuring tool for a given dimensional check
topics: 4.8 Gauges (welding, go/no-go, etc.), templates, scales, micrometers, calipers, special tools

media.title: Weld Gauges and Measuring Tools in Action
Read the Caliper and Micrometer Practice
Automated Visual Inspection System Block Diagram

modules.title: Applications, Discontinuities, and VT to Specific Procedures

lessons.title: Materials and Discontinuities: Employer-Defined Applications
objectives: Distinguish inherent, processing, and service-induced discontinuities
Describe visual testing applications for mineral-based, metallic, and organic-based materials
Recognize typical weld discontinuities visible at the surface
topics: 5.0 Employer-defined applications: inherent, processing, and service-induced discontinuities

media.title: Discontinuity Origins: Inherent, Processing, Service
Discontinuity Map by Material Family
Sort the Discontinuity: Inherent, Processing, or Service?

lessons.title: Performing VT to a Procedure: Parameters and Standardization
objectives: Identify inspection objectives, checkpoints, sampling plans, and inspection patterns from a documented procedure
Explain the purpose of test standards and standardization in visual testing
Follow a written VT procedure step by step

media.title: Reading a VT Procedure Before You Inspect
Sample Generic VT Procedure with Annotations
Plan an Inspection Pattern on a Sample Part

lessons.title: Classifying Indications and Documenting Results
objectives: Classify indications against given acceptance criteria
Complete a basic visual testing report with required documentation
Explain why complete and accurate records are essential
topics: 6.3 Classification of indications per acceptance criteria

media.title: From Indication to Disposition: Applying Acceptance Criteria
Fill Out a VT Report Form
Generic VT Report Template

levels.description: Covers the CP-105-2024 VT Level II topical outline: purpose and scope of VT, elements of vision and lighting, contrast and resolution, optics, VT equipment including measurement techniques, visual appearance of discontinuities, evaluation and reporting, codes and standards, and employer-specific topics. Emphasizes discontinuity cause, appearance, and detection.

modules.title: Purpose, Scope of VT, and Elements of Vision

lessons.title: Purpose and Scope of Visual Testing
objectives: Describe the quality control roles of VT for internal, customer, and authority oversight
Identify what VT looks for: discontinuities, shape and geometry deviations, and surface finish
Distinguish VT applied during manufacture from in-service VT

media.title: Where VT Fits in the Quality System
VT Scope: Manufacture vs. In-Service Inspection Points
Three Customers of a Visual Test

lessons.title: Mechanics of Vision, Adaptation, and Vision Limitations
objectives: Explain the mechanics of vision including adaptation and accommodation
Describe vision limitations from perception, environment, orientation, visual angle, and distance
Recognize how ophthalmic disorders, mental attitude, fatigue, and physiology affect inspection performance

media.title: Adaptation and Accommodation: Why Your Eyes Need Time
Visual Angle and Distance Geometry
Fatigue and Detection Rate Explorer

lessons.title: Vision Acuity Examinations and Charts
objectives: Describe common vision acuity tests and the charts used to administer them
Explain color and gray shade differentiation requirements for VT personnel
State why periodic vision examinations are required for certification

media.title: Near-Vision and Color Differentiation Exams Explained
Common Acuity Charts and What They Measure
Typical Vision Examination Requirements Summary

modules.title: Elements of Lighting, Contrast, and Resolution

lessons.title: Fundamentals of Light and Light Sources
objectives: Explain the fundamentals of light relevant to visual inspection
Compare incandescent and luminescent light sources including fluorescent, high-intensity discharge, LEDs, and lasers
Select an appropriate light source for a given inspection task
topics: 3.2.2 Luminescent radiators (fluorescent light, high-intensity discharge light, light-emitting diodes, lasers)

media.title: Light Sources for Inspection: From Incandescent to LED
Electromagnetic Spectrum and Visible Light
Compare Source Spectra and Color Rendering

lessons.title: Adequate Light Levels, Glare, and General Lighting Requirements
objectives: State adequate light level requirements for visual testing and how to verify them
Explain how glare contributes to inspector fatigue and missed indications
Apply general lighting requirements to an inspection setup

media.title: Measuring Light at the Inspection Surface
Position the Light: Eliminate Glare on a Curved Surface
Light Level Verification Checklist

lessons.title: Contrast and Resolution
objectives: Explain reflection at smooth and rough/textured surfaces and the law of illumination
Relate reflectivity, luminance, and luminous contrast to indication visibility
Describe how cleanliness, dark-field techniques, color, and surface geometry contribute to contrast
topics: 4.1 Reflection at smooth and rough/textured surfaces
4.7 Colors and contribution of colors to contrast

media.title: Contrast: The Physics of Seeing a Discontinuity
Specular vs. Diffuse Reflection and the Inverse Square Law
Contrast Lab: Vary Angle, Color, and Cleanliness
Dark-Field Contrast Technique Walkthrough

lessons.title: Optics Fundamentals: Transmission, Refraction, Prisms, and Lenses
objectives: Describe transmission of light through solid and liquid media
Explain refraction, refractive indexes of glasses, and how prisms change light direction
Explain how lenses focus and disperse light
topics: 5.1 Transmission of light through solid and liquid media
5.4 How prisms change the direction of light
5.5 How lenses focus and disperse light

media.title: Refraction, Prisms, and Lenses for Inspectors
Ray Tracing Through Prisms and Lenses
Bend the Beam: Refractive Index Explorer

lessons.title: Lens Trains, Fiber Optics, and Digital Technology
objectives: Describe lens optics and lens trains used in rigid scopes
Explain how fiber optics and fiber bundles transmit images and light
Summarize digitization and digital imaging technology in modern VT equipment

media.title: Inside the Scope: Lens Trains and Fiber Bundles
Coherent vs. Incoherent Fiber Bundles
Digital Imaging Terms Quick Reference

lessons.title: Generic Tools, Borescopes, Fiberscopes, and Video Borescopes
objectives: Apply generic tools such as magnifiers and mirrors to Level II inspection tasks
Compare rigid borescopes, fiberscopes, and video borescopes for access and image quality
Select the correct scope for a given access and resolution requirement
topics: 6.1 Generic tools such as magnifiers and mirrors

media.title: Choosing and Using Scopes at Level II
Scope Selection Scenarios: Pick the Right Instrument
Scope Capability Comparison Chart

lessons.title: Video Borescope Measurement Techniques and Specialized Systems
objectives: Describe comparison, shadow, stereo, and laser-based video borescope measurement techniques
Identify specialized inspection systems including push-tube cameras, pipe crawlers, and subsea remote cameras
Select an appropriate measurement technique for sizing an indication remotely

media.title: Measuring Through the Lens: Stereo and Shadow Techniques
Size the Indication: Remote Measurement Practice
Pipe Crawler and Push-Tube Camera System Layouts

modules.title: Visual Appearance of Discontinuities, Evaluation, and Reporting

lessons.title: Manufacturing and Inherent Discontinuities
objectives: Recognize the visual appearance of primary manufacturing discontinuities such as those in castings
Recognize secondary manufacturing discontinuities such as those in forgings
Describe inherent discontinuities and their origins in the material

media.title: Casting and Forging Discontinuities Up Close
Discontinuity Atlas: Manufacturing Origins
Identify the Discontinuity: Photo Flashcards

lessons.title: Service-Induced Discontinuities
objectives: Recognize discontinuities caused by mechanical loads such as fatigue cracking
Recognize discontinuities caused by thermal loads, corrosion, and abrasive wear
Relate service conditions to the expected discontinuity type and location
topics: 7.3.2 Caused by thermal loads (e.g., thermal shock)

media.title: Fatigue, Thermal Shock, Pitting, and Erosion: Field Examples
Service Damage Mechanisms and Visual Signatures
Match the Mechanism to the Damage Photo

lessons.title: Evaluation Schemes, Criteria, and Techniques
objectives: Apply the general evaluation scheme of ASTM E1316 terminology
Use verbal descriptions, comparison standards or catalogs, size-based criteria, and mixed criteria to evaluate indications
Perform evaluation by visual-tactile recognition, grading by comparison with a standard, and measurement
topics: 8.3.2 Grading by comparison with a standard

media.title: Evaluating an Indication Against Criteria
Grade the Surface: Comparison Standard Practice
Evaluation Criteria Types Summary Card

lessons.title: Reporting, Documentation, and Test Completion
objectives: Prepare technique reports, data reports, and image reports including sketches and photographs
Confirm completion of testing using a checklist
Maintain documentation that supports traceability and review
topics: 8.4.3 Image reports (sketches, hard-copy photo, or digital photo)
8.5 Completion of testing confirmed with a checklist

media.title: Building a Defensible VT Report
Complete the Inspection Checklist Exercise
Technique, Data, and Image Report Templates

modules.title: Codes and Standards, Employer-Specific Topics, and Glossary

lessons.title: Codes, Standards, and Specifications
objectives: Distinguish VT as an engineering task from VT as a technician task
Identify major US standards (e.g., ASME BPVC, AWS D1.1), European standards based on the PED, and ISO standards
Locate VT acceptance requirements within a governing code or standard
topics: 9.3 US standards (e.g., ASME BPVC, AWS D1.1)

media.title: Navigating Codes and Standards for VT
Code Hierarchy: Codes, Standards, Specifications, Procedures
Common VT Code References Quick Sheet

lessons.title: Employer-Specific Applications, Techniques, and Safety
objectives: Apply employer-specific applications, techniques, and lighting practices to representative inspection scenarios
Interpret employer specifications, materials tested, and special evaluation criteria
Follow applicable safety rules during visual testing operations

media.title: Tailoring VT to Your Application Area
Scenario Practice: Apply a Specification to a Test Object
VT Safety Rules Checklist

lessons.title: Glossary and Scope of Direct Visual Inspection
objectives: Use the VT glossary terminology from the Nondestructive Testing Handbook, Vol. 9: Visual Testing
Explain which outline topics may be deleted when VT is limited to direct visual inspection
topics: 11.1 Refer to Chapter 13 of ASNT, 2010, Nondestructive Testing Handbook, Vol. 9: Visual Testing, third edition
11.2 Topics may be deleted if the VT is only required to perform direct visual inspection

media.title: Key VT Glossary Terms Review
VT Glossary Study Sheet
Glossary Term Matching Game

modules.title: Materials, Processes, Test Variables, and Recording Media

lessons.title: Materials and Manufacturing Processes for Visual Testing
objectives: Use diagrams and drawings to plan and execute a visual test
Describe raw materials and primary process materials from ingot through plate, forging, casting, tubing, extrusion, and wire
Identify joining processes and joint configurations that visual testing must address

media.title: From Ingot to Component: Where VT Happens in Manufacturing
Primary Process Materials and Joining Process Map
Match the Material Form to Its Manufacturing Process

lessons.title: Fabricated Components, In-Service Materials, Coatings, and Other Applications
objectives: Apply VT techniques to fabricated components including pressure vessels, pumps, valves, fasteners, and piping systems
Recognize in-service degradation including wear and corrosion/erosion, and the use of microscopy for in-service evaluation
Describe VT of coatings and other applications such as ceramics, composites, plastics, and bearings, and relate codes and personnel qualification requirements to the work

media.title: Inspecting Fabricated Components: Vessels, Valves, and Piping
Coating Systems and In-Service Damage Mechanisms Atlas
Walkdown a Piping System: Find the Wear and Corrosion

lessons.title: Test Variables Affecting Visual Test Results
objectives: Explain how equipment, material, and discontinuity variables affect visual test results
Determine indication dimensions and apply sampling/scanning procedure controls
Describe how personnel (human factors) variables influence detection and interpretation
topics: 4.1 Equipment variables affecting test results, including type and intensity of light
4.2 Material variables affecting test results, including variations of surface finish
4.5 Sampling/scanning procedure variables affecting test results
4.7 Personnel (human factors) variables affecting test results

media.title: Why Results Vary: Equipment, Material, and Human Factors
Measure Depth, Width, and Length of an Indication
Test Variable Control Checklist

lessons.title: Recording Media, Photography Applications, and Personnel Certification
objectives: Apply photography to specific VT applications including welds, pressure vessels, pumps, valves, castings, forgings, and microcircuits
Identify code requirements (AWS, ASME, etc.) for audio/video records and use electronic and magnetic media for VT documentation
Summarize personnel qualification and certification requirements related to recording and reporting

media.title: Documenting What You See: Photography for VT Records
Build a Photo Record Package for a Weld Inspection
Recording Media and Code Documentation Requirements Sheet
